color-text-base.summary: Primary text color for body content and headings.
color-text-base.usage: Use for all default body copy, headings, and labels. Do not use for decorative text, disabled states, or secondary information — use text-subtle for those.
color-text-base.design: Anchors the text scale at maximum legibility. Darkest step in the warm-grey text ramp.

color-text-subtle.summary: Secondary text color for supporting and supplementary content.
color-text-subtle.usage: Use for captions, helper text, metadata, and secondary labels. Do not use for primary body copy or anything requiring full legibility — use text-base for those.
color-text-subtle.design: Mid-point in the text scale. Reduced contrast signals secondary importance while remaining accessible.

color-text-link.summary: Text color for interactive links and inline navigation.
color-text-link.usage: Use for hyperlinks, inline anchors, and any text that navigates or triggers an action. Do not use for non-interactive text — use text-base or text-subtle instead.
color-text-link.design: Draws from the blue brand scale to signal interactivity. Paired with text-link-hover to form the link interaction ramp.

color-text-link-hover.summary: Text color for links in their hovered state.
color-text-link-hover.usage: Use only as the hover state for text-link. Do not use for default link text or any non-interactive element.
color-text-link-hover.design: Lighter step in the blue brand scale, creating a visible but controlled shift from text-link on hover.

color-text-sale.summary: Text color for sale prices and promotional messaging.
color-text-sale.usage: Use exclusively for sale price labels and discount indicators. Do not use for error states, alerts, or general emphasis — those have dedicated tokens.
color-text-sale.design: Draws from the red brand scale. Reserved solely for commerce pricing contexts to preserve its signal strength.
